description: Parsing task with awkward edge cases (quoted quotes, missing fields, IPv6).
prompt:
Write a Python function `parse_line(line)` that parses one line of an nginx combined-format access log and returns a dict with keys: ip, timestamp, method, path, status (int), bytes (int), referrer, user_agent. Example line:

1.2.3.4 - - [03/Jul/2026:10:15:32 +0000] "GET /blog/post/ HTTP/1.1" 200 5123 "https://google.com/" "Mozilla/5.0"

Requirements: `bytes` may be '-' in the log, in which case return 0. The request field may be malformed (no HTTP version, or empty '""'); in that case set method and path to None but still parse the rest. The ip may be IPv6. referrer and user_agent are the quoted strings without their surrounding quotes; a '-' referrer stays as '-'. Return None for a line that does not match at all. Return only the final code in a single Python code block.

checker.test_code:
from solution import parse_line
r = parse_line('1.2.3.4 - - [03/Jul/2026:10:15:32 +0000] "GET /blog/post/ HTTP/1.1" 200 5123 "https://google.com/" "Mozilla/5.0"')
assert r["ip"] == "1.2.3.4" and r["method"] == "GET" and r["path"] == "/blog/post/"
assert r["status"] == 200 and r["bytes"] == 5123
assert r["referrer"] == "https://google.com/" and r["user_agent"] == "Mozilla/5.0"
r = parse_line('2001:db8::1 - - [03/Jul/2026:10:15:32 +0000] "" 400 - "-" "curl/8.0"')
assert r["ip"] == "2001:db8::1" and r["method"] is None and r["path"] is None
assert r["status"] == 400 and r["bytes"] == 0 and r["referrer"] == "-"
assert parse_line("not a log line") is None
print("ok")
